title: &group:[...] first-class groups, overlap+nest, DAG check
manager: First-class groups — `&mygroup: [a b c]` — that name a set of states and may overlap and nest, with a DAG check that forbids illegal cyclic containment. Groups are how FSL expresses hierarchy and cross-cutting sets (all the "error" states, all the "logged-in" states) without forcing everything into a strict tree, and they are the foundation the entire §19 cluster — cascade, boundary hooks, history, spread — builds on top of.
cs: `&group:[...]` first-class groups; overlap + nesting allowed; a containment DAG check rejects cycles; the base that the other group features (W6.81-.86) extend.
